resources.string: Come closer; the fever is warm enough for all of us
COME CLOSER. THE FEVER IS WARM ENOUGH FOR ALL.

The smiling caretaker touched each captive brow with a thumb of golden pus and bade them welcome. Around him the sick embraced, coughing red into one another’s sleeves, laughing through cracked blisters because no one had to shiver alone anymore. Even the gentle dead carried the new arrivals carefully through the marsh. Their cries sounded, to loving ears, like joy discovering that it had finally found a home.
KIND CONTAGION. PUS MARKS THE BROW. THE SICK EMBRACE. NO ONE SHIVERS ALONE. CRIES ARE WELCOME SONGS. THE FAMILY GROWS.
